title: Sanji and Zeff’s Debt Comes to the Surface
scene: On pages 17–18, the Baratie conflict focuses on Luffy, Sanji, Don Krieg, Gin, Zeff. After the fight, the Baratie shifts from survival to Sanji’s unresolved bond with Zeff and the cooks.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: It's a hundred years too early for a lil' egg- plant like you to be comparing my soup with yours!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I've cooked all around the world!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: This soup's pretty good if you ask me!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Whew, that was scary. He sure was stomping mad.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Everyone here knows how good Sanji's cooking is.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But if we didn't do that, that idiot would never listen to us...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Taking that lil' eggplant with you?

dialogue.speaker: Baratie cooks
dialogue.text: Jeez, that was more trouble than it was worth, owner Zeff!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: It was really hard to play Arlong.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Hey, give me another helping of this soup!

summary: This part keeps the Baratie battle centered on willpower, debt, hunger, and pride. The dialogue shows who still stands firm after Krieg’s violence and who is ready to break away from the restaurant’s old life.
